skill.name: firebase-development-debug
skill.description: Troubleshoot Firebase development issues across emulators, Firestore rules, Cloud Functions, Auth, and deployment workflows.
skill.summary: Guides systematic Firebase debugging from issue classification through testing and documentation.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found shell commands, localhost network access, and environment-file inspection guidance. Review confirms these are mostly legitimate Firebase debugging instructions, but reading .env files and killing processes creates elevated operational risk. No evidence found of malicious exfiltration, prompt injection, or hidden executable payloads.

medium_findings.title: Shell and Process Management Commands in Debug Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends shell commands for port checks, process termination, Firebase emulator control, exports, and build checks. These commands are normal for Firebase debugging, but they can disrupt local services or change emulator state if run without review. Verdict: true positive for operational command risk, not evidence of malicious execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in Markdown examples and align with the skill purpose. Risk remains because kill, Firebase CLI, and build commands can affect local processes and project state.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Environment File Inspection Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read functions/.env and hosting/.env.local while diagnosing auth configuration. This is a legitimate troubleshooting step, but it may expose secrets in the AI context or logs. Verdict: true positive for sensitive file access risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are directly shown and target environment files that commonly contain credentials. No network exfiltration path is present in the skill content.

low_findings.title: Localhost Network URL Flag Is Low Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to 127.0.0.1 for the Firebase Emulator UI. This supports local debugging and does not indicate external communication. Verdict: false positive for external network risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a loopback address and appears under instructions for opening the Emulator UI. There is no evidence of data being sent to an external service.

low_findings.title: Descriptive Text Triggered Heuristic False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance flags map to descriptive Firebase debugging text, not cryptographic calls or host reconnaissance code. Verdict: false positive for these blocker findings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and an auth troubleshooting table entry. I did not find evidence of cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance logic.

low_findings.title: Reference Backticks Are Not Command Execution
low_findings.description: Several static command findings point to backticked skill names and documentation paths. These references do not execute code by themselves. Verdict: false positive for Ruby or shell backtick execution on these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown inline code for references, paths, or skill routing. It is not executable Ruby syntax in this context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Guidance Combined With Secret File Reads
dangerous_patterns.description: The static dangerous-combination heuristic is partially supported because the workflow includes shell commands and .env file reads. It is downgraded because the network endpoint is localhost and there is no evidence of exfiltration or hidden code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination exists at the instruction level, but semantic context shows local Firebase debugging. The lack of external network transfer lowers confidence in malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Firebase development failures can be hard to isolate across emulators, rules, functions, auth, and deployment. This skill guides a structured debugging workflow with focused checks and repeatable fixes.
actual_capabilities: Classifies Firebase errors by emulator, rules, functions, auth, or deployment area.
Guides checks for emulator logs, port conflicts, and Emulator UI diagnostics.
Recommends Firestore Rules Playground tests for permission failures.
Helps inspect Cloud Functions errors with targeted logging and reproduction steps.
Reviews Firebase deployment configuration and predeploy build checks.
Prompts documentation of symptoms, root cause, fix, and prevention.
limitations: It provides diagnostic guidance, not automatic fixes for every Firebase issue.
It may ask to inspect local environment files that can contain secrets.
It assumes the Firebase CLI and emulator tools are already available.
It does not replace production incident response or cloud security review.

use_cases.title: Resolve Emulator Startup Failures
use_cases.description: Diagnose port conflicts, emulator initialization errors, and lost local emulator state.

use_cases.title: Debug Rules and Auth Errors
use_cases.description: Trace permission denied errors through auth context, request data, and Rules Playground simulation.

use_cases.title: Investigate Deployment Breakages
use_cases.description: Check Firebase deployment logs, project configuration, targets, and predeploy build steps.

prompt_templates.title: Classify My Firebase Error
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Firebase development error. Help me classify whether it is emulator, rules, functions, auth, or deployment related.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Emulator Startup
prompt_templates.prompt: My Firebase emulators do not start. Walk me through safe checks for ports, logs, and emulator state before restarting.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Permission Denied
prompt_templates.prompt: A Firestore request returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Help me test auth context, request data, and rules evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Deployment Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: My Firebase deploy fails with unclear output. Build a diagnosis plan covering logs, firebase.json, targets, and predeploy hooks.

output_examples.input: My emulator reports that a port is already in use.
output: Identifies this as an emulator startup issue.
Checks the affected port and the running process before any termination step.
Recommends exporting or preserving emulator data before restart when relevant.

output_examples.input: A client write to Firestore fails with permission denied.
output: Classifies the issue as a rules or auth problem.
Guides a Rules Playground simulation with operation, path, auth context, and request data.
Separates Admin SDK behavior from client SDK rule enforcement.

output_examples.input: Firebase deploy fails after a predeploy hook runs.
output: Reviews debug logs and Firebase project configuration.
Runs the build step locally to isolate the failing hook.
Documents the root cause and prevention notes after the fix.

best_practices: Confirm the issue category before running commands or changing configuration.
Redact secrets before sharing environment file contents or debug logs.
Export emulator state before restarts that could remove local test data.
anti_patterns: Killing processes without confirming the process owner and project context.
Pasting full .env contents into chat or issue trackers without redaction.
Changing rules, auth, and function code at the same time during diagnosis.

faq.question: What Firebase problems does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers emulator startup, Firestore rules, Cloud Functions, Auth emulator, and deployment failures.

faq.question: Does it run Firebase commands automatically?
faq.answer: It provides command guidance. Users should review commands before execution, especially process termination commands.

faq.question: Is it safe to inspect .env files?
faq.answer: Inspect them only when needed and redact secrets before sharing output with any AI tool or collaborator.

faq.question: Can it debug production Firebase incidents?
faq.answer: It is designed for development workflows. Production incidents need stricter access control and incident procedures.

faq.question: Does it help with Firestore rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Rules Playground checks with operation type, document path, auth context, and request data.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
